To find a median, we need fast access to the middle of a sorted array.
Use two heaps: a Max-Heap to store the smaller half of numbers, and a Min-Heap to store the larger half.
Keep the sizes of the heaps balanced such that their size difference is at most 1.
If total count is odd, the median is the root of the larger heap. If even, the median is the average of the roots of both heaps.
